observations.recommendation:
1.9 We recommended that management re-constitute a new Inventory Committee to
implement the one-time cleansing of PPE in accordance with COA Circular 2020-
006, conduct inventory taking, and submit the required RPCPPE and other
relevant reports and schedules to the audit team.
1.10 We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Property and
Supply Officer conduct regular reconciliation of property records, investigate
discrepancies noted, initiate appropriate processes, and make necessary
adjustments in the books to reflect the correct balances of PPE.
1.11 Finally, it is recommended that management give priority to the implementation
of the one-time cleansing of PPE and take good advantage of COA Circular No.
2020-006 in order to arrive at reliable and verifiable year end balances.

2.4. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant regularly and properly
compute and provide for the corresponding depreciation for all depreciable
assets of the municipality in order to present accurate balances of PPE
accounts in the financial statements.

3.8. We recommended that the Inventory Committee prepare a Report on the
Physical Count of Local Road Network using the prescribed format in COA
Circular No. 2015-008.
3.9. Further, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain
subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network
and prepare a lapsing schedule for the computation of depreciation for each
component at the end of the year. Appropriate disclosure on Road Network
System shall likewise be included in the Notes to the Financial Statements.
3.10. Finally, it is recommended that the GSO keep and maintain Local Road
Network Property Cards for all the roads and its components, and render a
Report on Local Road Network at year end.

4.7. We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to make the
necessary corrections by drawing the following journal entries:
Appropriate PPE Accounts ₱7,547,837.00
Repairs and Maintenance – Expense Accounts ₱7,547,837.00
To adjust capitalizable cost
#
Depreciation Expense xxx
Accumulated Depreciation xxx
To adjust unrecorded depreciation

5.7. We recommended and the Municipal Engineer agreed to submit to the
Municipal Accountant the documents necessary to support the recognition of
the completed projects. The Municipal Accountant likewise agreed to take up
the necessary entries reclassifying the CIP – Buildings and Other Structures
to the appropriate PPE accounts and the corresponding depreciation that
should have already been provided for the assets.

6.8. We recommended that the Municipal Assessor and Municipal Treasurer
coordinate to improve the functionality of both the ETRACS and RPT database
systems to include automatic updating and validation in order to reconcile the
existing differences in real property assessments, and provide the Municipal
Accountant with the updated, validated, and certified list containing the name
of taxpayers and the corresponding amount of taxes due and collectible for
appropriate adjustment/s in the books of accounts.

7.11 We recommended that:
1) The Municipal Accountant record the purchases of supplies and
inventories using the perpetual inventory method pursuant to Section 114
of NGAS Manual for LGUs Volume I; and
2) The GSO to prepare the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued
(SSMI) and submit it the same to the Municipal Accountant as basis in
recording the issuances/expense pursuant to Section 121 of the Manual.

7.12 We further recommended that the GSO or the designated Inventory
Committee conduct a physical count of all inventories at hand and to
reconcile the results of such count with the records of the Municipal
Accountant who shall take up the appropriate adjusting entry/ies, if
warranted.

8.9. We recommended and management agreed that:
a. The Municipal Accountant and Agricultural Officer prepare and
maintain the ledger cards and stock/property cards respectively, for the
Breeding Stocks and reconcile their balances regularly.
b. The Municipal Agriculture Officer regularly monitor the condition of the
livestock/breeding stocks under her supervision and update her records
for easy reference.
c. A report on the physical count of the reported Breeding Stocks be
prepared and submitted not later than January 31 of each year.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to ascertain whether the checks were claimed or not and follow the procedure provided in Section 59, NGAS Vol. I cited as follows:

A spoiled or stale check shall be marked cancelled on its face and reported as follows:
Xxx.
For stale checks which have been unclaimed and thus, the original DV and supporting documents are still with the Treasurer, the cancelled check shall be presented in the RCI after the last check issued for the period indicated in the report. The original DV and supporting documents shall be returned to the Accountant who shall prepare a JEV to record the transaction as Accounts Payable.

For checks which became spoiled or stale in the hands of the payee and which require replacement, a new check may be issued upon submission of the spoiled or stale check to the Treasurer. A certified copy of the DV shall be requested from the Auditor for presentation to the Administrator/Local Chief Executive who shall countersign the check. The cancelled check shall be reported and attached to the RCI prepared at the period of cancellation. The replacement check shall also be reported chronologically in the RCI.

We recommend that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to ascertain whether the checks were claimed or not and follow the procedure provided in Section 59, NGAS Vol. I cited as follows:
A spoiled or stale check shall be marked cancelled on its face and reported as follows:
Xxx.
For stale checks which have been unclaimed and thus, the original DV and supporting documents are still with the Treasurer, the cancelled check shall be presented in the RCI after the last check issued for the period indicated in the report. The original DV and supporting documents shall be returned to the Accountant who shall prepare a JEV to record the transaction as Accounts Payable.
For checks which became spoiled or stale in the hands of the payee and which require replacement, a new check may be issued upon submission of the spoiled or stale check to the Treasurer. A certified copy of the DV shall be requested from the Auditor for presentation to the Administrator/Local Chief Executive who shall countersign the check. The cancelled check shall be reported and attached to the RCI prepared at the period of cancellation. The replacement check shall also be reported chronologically in the RCI..

We recommend Management to enforce the periodic submission of the required financial reports and documents within the prescribed period by looking into the root cause of the delayed preparation and submission of said reports and immediately take the necessary action to solve this issue.
We further recommend the Local Chief Executive to initiate the filing of appropriate actions to officials who unjustifiably refuse to comply with the above stated regulations.
